Lithocarpus is a genus of trees in the beech family, Fagaceae, and Lithocarpus species are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Lithocarpus species, including Lithocarpus longinux, have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, and have been used to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for their medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Lithocarpus species are often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, and are also used in the production of herbal remedies such as syrups, tonics, and salves.
